domain:
A Person has a name and an identifier. Every Person is the child of another Person and can have an indeterminate number of children (however, there will be one Person who is not the child of any other Person). A Person has an indeterminate number of ancestors (the ancestors of a Person are their father and the ancestors of their father). We will be interested in knowing the degree of each ancestor of a Person (1 for the father, 2 for the father's father, etc.).

A Resource has a name. There are two types of Resources, such as Files and Folders. Every resource belongs to a Folder and cannot exist outside of a Folder (however, there will be one Folder that does not follow that rule, the root Folder does not belong to any other Folder). Each Folder contains an indeterminate number of Resources.

A User is a Person who, in addition to a name and an identifier, also has a password to access an indeterminate number of Resources, and each Resource can be accessed by an indeterminate number of Users. When a User can access a resource, we will be interested in knowing if they can view it and if they can edit it.

questions.question:
Should ancestor link be modeled as a separate class associated with Person, or as a simple representation within Person?

For example:
1. Ancestor link is a separate class with attributes: degree. This allows users to query and filter by individual ancestor link properties.
2. Ancestor link is modeled as a simple represntation in Person. This is suitable when ancestor link details are not needed.
Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: The AncestorLink is a separate class with its own attributes.
questions.option_2: Option 2: The ancestor link is a simple representation in Person.
questions.selected: Option 1: The AncestorLink is a separate class with its own attributes.

questions.question:
Should the relationship between Person and AncestorLink be modeled as a composition or as an association?

For example:
1. Use composition, AncestorLink would depend on Person for its existence.
2. Use association, AncestorLink could exist independently of Person, and can also be linked to multiple people.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Composition is needed because class AncestorLink depends on Person.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Association is sufficient because class AncestorLink exists independently of Person.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of Person in the relationship with AncestorLink?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, an ancestor link can exist without any associated person.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, an ancestor link must have at least one associated person.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, AncestorLink can exist without Person.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality,AncestorLink must have at least one Person.
questions.selected: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, AncestorLink can exist without Person.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of AncestorLink in the relationship with Person?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, a person can exist without any associated ancestor link.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, a person must have at least one associated ancestor link.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Person can exist without AncestorLink.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality,Person must have at least one AncestorLink.
questions.selected: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality,Person must have at least one AncestorLink.

questions.question:
What is the maximum cardinality of AncestorLink in the relationship with Person?

For example:
1. Maximum cardinality of one, a person is associated with exactly one ancestor link.
2. Maximum cardinality of many, a person can be associated with multiple ancestor links.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: One as maximun cardinality, each person links to one ancestor link.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Many as maximun cardinality, each person can link to multiple ancestor links.
questions.selected: Option 2: Many as maximun cardinality, each person can link to multiple ancestor links.

questions.question:
Should views be modeled as a subclass of access, or is it better to keep a simple boolean like access in view to distinguish them?
Consider whether views have or will have distinct attributes or behavior.

For example:
1. View is a subclass of access. This allows views to have their own specific attributes and methods.
2. View is an attribute in class access. All instances share the same structure, with the attribute indicating the type.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Use inheritance, View as a subclass ofaccess.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Use view as a boolean attribute of  access.
questions.selected: Option 2: Use view as a boolean attribute of  access.

questions.question:
Should edits be modeled as a subclass of access, or is it better to keep a simple boolean like access in edit to distinguish them?
Consider whether edits have or will have distinct attributes or behavior.

For example:
1. Edit is a subclass of access. This allows edits to have their own specific attributes and methods.
2. Edit is an attribute in class access. All instances share the same structure, with the attribute indicating the type.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Use inheritance, Edit as a subclass ofaccess.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Use edit as a boolean attribute of  access.
questions.selected: Option 2: Use edit as a boolean attribute of  access.

questions.question:
Should the relationship between User and Access be modeled as a composition or as an association?

For example:
1. Use composition, Access would depend on User for its existence.
2. Use association, Access could exist independently of User, and can also be linked to multiple users.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Composition is needed because class Access depends on User.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Association is sufficient because class Access exists independently of User.
questions.selected: Option 1: Composition is needed because class Access depends on User.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of User in the relationship with Access?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, an access can exist without any associated user.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, an access must have at least one associated user.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Access can exist without User.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality,Access must have at least one User.
questions.selected: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality,Access must have at least one User.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of Resource in the relationship with Access?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, an access can exist without any associated resource.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, an access must have at least one associated resource.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Access can exist without Resource.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality,Access must have at least one Resource.
questions.selected: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality,Access must have at least one Resource.

questions.question:
What is the maximum cardinality of Resource in the relationship with Access?

For example:
1. Maximum cardinality of one, an access is associated with exactly one resource.
2. Maximum cardinality of many, an access can be associated with multiple resources.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: One as maximun cardinality, each access links to one resource.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Many as maximun cardinality, each access can link to multiple resources.
questions.selected: Option 2: Many as maximun cardinality, each access can link to multiple resources.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of Access in the relationship with Resource?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, a resource can exist without any associated access.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, a resource must have at least one associated access.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Resource can exist without Access.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality,Resource must have at least one Access.
questions.selected: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality,Resource must have at least one Access.

questions.question:
What is the maximum cardinality of Access in the relationship with Resource?

For example:
1. Maximum cardinality of one, a resource is associated with exactly one access.
2. Maximum cardinality of many, a resource can be associated with multiple accesses.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: One as maximun cardinality, each resource links to one access.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Many as maximun cardinality, each resource can link to multiple accesses.
questions.selected: Option 2: Many as maximun cardinality, each resource can link to multiple accesses.

questions.question:
Should Access be modeled as an association class linking User and Resource, or as a regular class with two separate associations?

For example:
1. Association class: Access exists only as part of the relationship between User and Resource. A access cannot exist without the link between User and Resource.
2. Regular class: Access is a class that has links to User and Resource. In this case multiple accesses can exist for every User and Resource.


Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Association class, Access as association class linking User and Resource.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Regular class, Access as separate class with two associations.
questions.selected: Option 1: Association class, Access as association class linking User and Resource.
